title: vpn_failure
description: The corporate VPN gateway fails, locking out all remote workers from corporate systems.

timeline.title: VPN Gateway Unresponsive
timeline.description: Remote workers begin reporting VPN connection failures. The gateway stops accepting connections.

timeline.title: Mass Disconnection
timeline.description: All active VPN sessions are terminated. 500 remote workers lose access to corporate systems.

timeline.title: Gateway Health Check
timeline.description: The VPN gateway appliance is physically accessible. CPU is at 100% and the process is unresponsive.

timeline.title: Failover Gateway Assessment
timeline.description: Engineers check whether a secondary VPN gateway or cloud-based backup is available.

timeline.title: Emergency VPN Restored
timeline.description: Secondary gateway activated or cloud VPN provisioned. Workers begin reconnecting.

timeline.title: Primary Gateway Restored
timeline.description: Primary gateway is repaired. Traffic migrated back. All remote workers have connectivity.

node-1.title: VPN Gateway Failure
node-1.situation: The primary VPN gateway has stopped accepting connections. 500 remote workers cannot access any corporate resources. The gateway appliance is consuming 100% CPU and its management interface is unresponsive. You have console access to the physical appliance.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Notify all remote workers of the outage, assess the gateway state via console, and immediately begin activating the secondary VPN gateway to restore connectivity while the primary is investigated.

options.label: B
options.text: Reboot the primary VPN gateway immediately without notifying workers or activating a failover, causing additional disruption.

options.label: C
options.text: Ask all remote workers to use personal tools to access work systems while the VPN is down.

node-2.title: Secondary Gateway Activation
node-2.situation: The secondary VPN gateway exists but its configuration is 2 months out of date. Some user accounts and access policies added in the last 2 months may not work. The alternative is to quickly provision a cloud VPN that can pull the current user directory from Active Directory.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Provision the cloud VPN using the current Active Directory integration to ensure all users can authenticate, accepting a slightly longer setup time for a fully functional failover.

options.label: B
options.text: Activate the stale secondary gateway knowing 2 months of user changes will be missing and some workers will still be locked out.

node-3.title: User Communication and Reconnection
node-3.situation: The cloud VPN is operational. Workers need to connect using a new endpoint URL and install a new VPN client profile. You need to communicate this to 500 workers efficiently.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Send a mass communication via email and Slack (accessible without VPN) with clear step-by-step reconnection instructions, and set up a help desk queue for workers who need individual assistance.

options.label: B
options.text: Post instructions only on the internal intranet, which requires VPN to access, leaving workers unable to find reconnection guidance.

node-4.title: Primary Gateway Repair
node-4.situation: Most workers have reconnected via the cloud VPN. The primary gateway issue has been diagnosed: a runaway IKE negotiation process caused by a bug in the firmware version. A firmware update resolves the issue and a patch is available.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Apply the firmware patch to the primary gateway in a test environment, validate it, then apply to production during a low-traffic window, and migrate users back gradually while keeping the cloud VPN as a standby.

options.label: B
options.text: Apply the firmware patch to the primary production gateway immediately without testing to restore it as quickly as possible.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You managed the VPN gateway failure by quickly activating a cloud VPN failover with current AD integration, communicating recovery steps to 500 remote workers via accessible channels, and safely patching the primary gateway before returning it to service.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
